title: The Saturday Market
note: SAMPLE authoring template (original content, not transcribed from any source). The generator picks N values per category, samples a unique solution, emits clues from clueTemplates honoring the tier's indirection + compound gates, verifies uniqueness (is_unique), then renders story + numbered clues. Rich COMPOUND puzzles are GENERATED and verified-unique, never hand-authored - see plan sec 19.
narrativeTemplate: Every Saturday, {n} friends run neighbouring stalls at the riverside craft market, watched over by old {mentor}, who has kept the market ledger for forty years and still records every sale in {ink} ink. This week each friend brought a different handmade craft and settled on a different price. {mentor} has misplaced the ledger page - match every friend to the craft they made and the price they charged.

indirection.note: How an operand is named in a clue. 'name' -> 'Ana'; 'attribute' -> 'the potter' (uses the value's refPhrase). Gated up by tier so hard tiers read indirectly like a printed puzzle.

variants: Whatever {a_ref} brought, it was not the {b_label}.
{a_ref}'s takings came to {delta} dollars more than {b_ref}'s.
{a_ref} did not charge less than {bound} dollars.

oneOf.note: disjunction: entity a is one of {x, y}
variants: {a_ref} could not decide between the {x_label} and the {y_label}, and set out just one.
{a_ref} brought either the {x_label} or the {y_label} - not both.

oneEachOf.note: of two entities x and y, one satisfies fact A and the other fact B
variants: Of the {x_ref} and the {y_ref}, one {a_phrase} and the other {b_phrase}.
